title: [CI:BUILD] Copr: create tests-e2e subpackage
body:
On the container-selinux upstream repo, I'd like to run Podman's tests for selinux on every PR, to ensure any updates to container-selinux do not break Podman.

Ref: https://github.com/containers/container-selinux/issues/211

This commit creates a `podman-tests-e2e` subpackage which can be installed much like one would install `podman-tests`.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

@edsantiago @rhatdan PTAL.

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Good luck! I tried this years ago and wasted over two days trying to get the tests to run.

> Good luck! I tried this years ago and wasted over two days trying to get the tests to run.

@edsantiago  ack, let's see how packit performs: https://github.com/containers/container-selinux/pull/215
@cevich are there any `skip-windows`, `skip-debian` tags I could use for fedora specific changes such as this one? I guess I could create them if not.

> are there any skip-windows, skip-debian tags

There are not.  They slightly scare me because relationships aren't always obvious.  For example, documentation changes need to run the Windows installer test (IIRC).

That said, if you would like to add some mechanism for this I'm not opposed to it.  Though on the surface it sounds like a road toward pain.
Closing as this isn't needed for now.
